## [Management Faculty Research](https://mds.marshall.edu/mgmt_faculty)

# [Tele-ICU in the Unites States: Is a cost-effective model?](https://mds.marshall.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1244&context=mgmt_faculty)

## Authors

[**Michael Robie**, *Marshall University*](https://mds.marshall.edu/do/search/?q=author%3A%22Michael%20Robie%22&start=0&context=2101447)  
[**Stephanie Cole**, *Marshall University*](https://mds.marshall.edu/do/search/?q=author%3A%22Stephanie%20Cole%22&start=0&context=2101447)  
[**Archana Suwal**, *Marshall University*](https://mds.marshall.edu/do/search/?q=author%3A%22Archana%20Suwal%22&start=0&context=2101447)  
[**Alberto Coustasse**, *Marshall University*](https://mds.marshall.edu/do/search/?q=%28author%3A%22Alberto%20Coustasse%22%20AND%20-bp_author_id%3A%5B%2A%20TO%20%2A%5D%29%20OR%20bp_author_id%3A%28%2219b3e82a-462c-4c9c-9be5-444020e21acb%22%29&start=0&context=2101447)[Follow](https://network.bepress.com/api/follow/subscribe?user=OTdhYjhmOGM1ZThmM2QwZQ%3D%3D&institution=MDU3N2Q2NmIzMzkyMWEyNw%3D%3D&format=html "Follow Alberto Coustasse")

## Document Type

Article

## Publication Date

2-23-2022

## Abstract

**Introduction:** The United States Society of Critical Care Medicine has announced a shortage of all critical care intensivists due to the increased need for critical care for the aging population with comorbidities and improved life expectancy. This shortage has led to tele-ICU programs that have allowed intensivists to care for patients simultaneously remotely.

**Methods:** This study aimed to assess the potential for the hospital implementation of tele-ICU to determine its overall healthcare cost-effectiveness. The methodology was a review that followed a systematic search approach utilizing 42 sources.

**Results:** The study findings showed that tele-ICU contributed to reduced hospital LOS by 36% and positive financial outcomes of return of investment and cost-effectiveness. These reduced LOS stay times have allowed for savings up to $11.5 million.

**Discussion:** The Tele-ICU program's implementation has positively impacted financial and clinical areas, reducing mortality and reduced hospital LOS. Furthermore, it has reduced improved patient safety, care quality, and decreased ventilator days, contributing to a cost-efficient technology.

## Comments

This is the authors’ peer-reviewed manuscript. The version of record is available from the publisher at <https://doi.org/10.1080/20479700.2022.2040877>. Copyright © 2022 Taylor & Francis. All rights reserved.

## Recommended Citation

Michael Robie, E., Cole, S., Suwal, A., & Coustasse, A. (2022). Tele-ICU in the Unites States: Is a cost-effective model?. International Journal of Healthcare Management, 1-8.

URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3103644/

SUMMARY:
After the implementation of the tele-ICU, the hospital daily cost increased from $4,302 to $5,340 (24%), the hospital cost per case from $21,967 to $31,318 (43%), and the cost per patient from $20,231 to $25,846 (28%) .

enriched_points.expanded_content: Tele-ICU systems have shown a 36% reduction in hospital length of stay (LOS), leading to substantial cost savings and improved resource utilization.
Implementing Tele-ICU can yield financial benefits, including potential savings of up to $11.5 million through reduced hospital stays and enhanced care efficiency.
Sustaining Tele-ICU programs requires ongoing evaluation of costs versus benefits, ensuring they remain financially viable while continuing to enhance patient care quality.
